findings.title: Redacted cast failures can still expose the offending value through the copied reason text
findings.why: This should stay redacted end-to-end: the new `ErrCastValue` contract only marks the `received value` argument for redaction, but `LogKVConvertFailed` also copies `err.Error()` into the `Reason` field verbatim.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: When `tidb_enable_redact_log` is enabled, import/type-error records can still leak raw input data from cast failures, so operators do not actually get the compatibility/safety guarantee that the new redacted error format suggests.
findings.evidence: `ErrCastValue` redacts only argument index 2 in `pkg/lightning/common/errors.go`, while `pkg/lightning/backend/kv/base.go` sets `reason := err.Error()`. The updated regression case in `pkg/executor/importer/kv_encode_test.go:121` shows a real overflow error whose reason contains the original datum (`constant 10000000 overflows tinyint`), so a redacted message would still expose that value through `Reason:`.
findings.change_request: Can we redact or sanitize the copied `reason` before building `ErrCastValue`, instead of passing through the raw cast error text? Add a case for a redact-enabled real cast failure (for example the overflow path) to ensure the final error string never reintroduces the offending datum through `Reason:`.
